data.title: Crypto exchanges will need Russian office to avoid ban by Moscow
data.description:
Cryptocurrency exchanges will have to set up a subsidiary in Russia and abide by its laws to be allowed to work there, the country’s monetary authority has made that clear. The statement comes amid fears that Russian authorities are planning to cut off access to global trading platforms for digital assets as soon as they […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/crypto-exchanges-will-need-russian-office-to-avoid-ban/

data.title: Inside DeFi 005: ️ Base shuns Optimism, Discord’s KYC disaster
data.description:
Welcome back to Inside DeFi Coinbase-founded Base has decided to shun optimism, DeFi platforms are shutting down, and Discord has caused a new crypto controversy around its use of age verification technology. Elsewhere, Artificial Intelligence (AI) benchmarks suggest that AI is more useful to exploit protocols than patch them. Optimism’s glass is looking (more than) half empty Coinbase’s Base network announced this week that it would be pursuing a “new, unified stack,” leaving Optimism’s OP Stack infrastructure and moving to its own Base Chain. Faster shipping, a simpler architecture, and Ethereum alignment are the three objectives Base claims for the decision
source: https://protos.com/inside-defi-005-%f0%9f%8c%aa%ef%b8%8f-base-shuns-optimism-discords-kyc-disaster/

data.title: Codelco Anticipates Prolonged Reduced Output at Major Copper Mine
data.description: Codelco, Chile's state-owned copper mining company, foresees that production at its largest copper mine will remain at current reduced levels for several years. Bloomberg posted on X that this projection comes as the company deals with the extensive repercussions of Chile's most fatal mining accident in decades. The accident has significantly impacted operations, leading to a prolonged period of decreased output. Codelco is working to address the challenges posed by this incident, which has affected its production capabilities and overall performance.

data.title: Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone Attracts Private Capital
data.description: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) is increasingly attracting private capital due to its scalable industrial capacity and AI-ready infrastructure. FactSet posted on X, highlighting the zone's potential to enhance cross-border trade corridors and facilitate strategic, export-oriented deals. The JS-SEZ is positioned as a key player in fostering economic growth and investment opportunities in the region.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Criticizes Federal Reserve Chair
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed criticism towards the Federal Reserve Chair, describing him as highly incompetent. According to ChainCatcher, Trump stated that the Chair favors high interest rates. This comment reflects ongoing tensions between the President and the Federal Reserve over monetary policy.

data.title: Trump’s Tariffs Just Collapsed—and Crypto Could Be Next
data.description:
In a 6–3 decision, the Supreme Court of the United States ruled that tariffs imposed during the Trump administration violated federal law. Specifically, the Court found that the executive branch exceeded its authority when it imposed broad trade duties under emergency statutes. com
source: https://techgaged.com/trumps-tariffs-just-collapsed-and-crypto-could-be-next/

data.title: U.S. President Trump Considers Trade Law Options Amid Economic Tensions
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has referenced several trade laws as potential measures to address ongoing economic issues. According to Jin10, Trump mentioned the Trade Expansion Act and the Trade Act of 1974 as frameworks for possible action. He highlighted Sections 232, 122, 201, and 301 as viable options, while noting that Section 338 could also be considered, albeit with a longer procedural timeline. These considerations come amid heightened trade tensions and economic challenges.

data.title: STOCKS | US Stock Market Rallies as Supreme Court Overturns Emergency Tariffs
data.description: The US stock market experienced a rally following the Supreme Court's decision to declare emergency tariffs unconstitutional, alleviating a significant inflationary concern. According to NS3.AI, this development comes amid sluggish fourth-quarter GDP growth and rising inflation, which highlight potential stagflation risks. Despite these economic challenges, the market reacted positively, with the Technology sector and Alphabet leading the gains. However, uncertainty persists as the administration may explore alternative tariff measures, and ongoing inflation pressures continue to support expectations for interest rate hikes.

data.title: Hungary to Block EU Loan to Ukraine Over Russian Oil Shipments
data.description: Hungary has announced its decision to block the release of a €90 billion loan to Ukraine, which was agreed upon by the European Union. Bloomberg posted on X that Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto stated the move is contingent on the resumption of Russian oil shipments through Hungary. The decision underscores the complex geopolitical dynamics at play, as Hungary leverages its position within the EU to influence energy and financial policies. The situation highlights the ongoing tensions and negotiations involving EU member states, Ukraine, and Russia.

data.title: Team Canada Secures Dramatic Victory Over Finland in Hockey Semifinals
data.description: Team Canada staged a remarkable comeback in the men's hockey semifinals, overcoming a 2-0 deficit to defeat Finland with a last-minute goal. New York Times posted on X, highlighting the thrilling nature of the match. With just 35 seconds remaining, Canada managed to score the decisive goal, securing their place in the Olympic gold medal game. They now await the outcome of the USA vs. Slovakia match to determine their final opponent.

data.title: No Perfect Scores Yet in Latest Edition of Pointed
data.description: The latest edition of the game Pointed has seen only 24 players achieve a perfect score. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenge of joining the exclusive 190-point club. Players are encouraged to test their skills and aim for perfection in this competitive game.

data.title: President Trump Claims Tariffs Helped Settle Eight Wars
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced that he successfully settled eight wars through the strategic use of tariffs. Crypto Rover posted on X, highlighting Trump's statement about the effectiveness of tariffs in resolving international conflicts. The President did not specify which wars were settled or provide further details on the negotiations involved. This announcement comes amid ongoing discussions about the role of tariffs in international diplomacy and economic policy.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan Advocates Shift to Repo Market Rates
data.description: Federal Reserve official Logan has expressed a preference for the central bank to focus on repo market rates instead of the federal funds rate. According to ChainCatcher, this perspective suggests a strategic shift in how the Federal Reserve might approach its monetary policy objectives.

data.title: New Crypto Bill Reportedly Targets Market Manipulation
data.description:
Rumors around a definitive timeline for the U. S. Crypto Market Structure Bill, often described as a refined successor to FIT21 or the Digital Asset Market Structure Act, have intensified following recent regulatory shifts
source: https://www.ethnews.com/new-crypto-bill-reportedly-targets-market-manipulation/
